element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic throughout; foundational skill before weapon pickups
Supplement basic attacks, available throughout levels

element_introduction_order.element: Jump kick and slide kick variants
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Extended mobility and attack options available from start
Grotesque monster encounters punctuate each stage; escalate in threat
Primary movement axis; establishes forward momentum through destroyed architecture
Thematic goal: locating the 'land of the dead' mansion drives narrative tension across all 8 stages

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing enemy waves within a stage section
typical_tension_triggers: Grotesque monster design and gore presentation
Narrative reminder of Jennifer's peril and mask temptation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Short single-player campaign with escalating horror intensity; repetitive combat punctuated by boss gauntlet; reliance on shock value and grotesque imagery for sustained tension

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden mansion discovery contextually signaled through stage progression; Japanese version clarity ('find the hidden house') vs. Western confusion about location

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate; introductory combat with basic attacks and early weapon pickups
notable_spikes: Boss encounters at end of each of 8 stages escalate in difficulty
Game includes difficulty setting option (new addition vs. original)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 8 stages concludes with a grotesque monster boss fight, establishing rhythm of accumulation and payoff
Stages feature destroyed/haunted architecture: Stage 1 opens on charred ruins of West Mansion before scrolling reveal of intact hidden mansion
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 1 (West Mansion ruins transitioning to hidden house)

notable_level_patterns.description: Two-dimensional side-scrolling progression with enemy encounters and weapon pickups distributed along horizontal movement path
Scattered weapons throughout levels encourage environmental exploration within forward momentum; supports repeated playthroughs

sources_used: Wikipedia - Splatterhouse 2 (gameplay, premise, 8-stage structure, boss encounters, regional differences, narrative context)
